title: ICE Agent Christian Castro Charged with Assault and Falsely Reporting a Crime in Minneapolis Shooting
excerpt: Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty charged ICE agent Christian Castro with four counts of second-degree assault and one count of falsely reporting a crime in connection with the January 14 nonfatal shooting of Venezuelan immigrant Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis through the front door of a Minneapolis home. City surveillance video contradicted Castro's account that he had been attacked with a shovel for three minutes.
content:
On May 18, 2026, Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty charged ICE agent Christian Castro, 52, with four counts of second-degree assault and one count of falsely reporting a crime stemming from the January 14, 2026 shooting of Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis in Minneapolis.

  • The state alleged Castro shot Sosa-Celis through the front door of a Minneapolis home — with four adults and two children inside — after a struggle that lasted approximately 12 seconds, directly contradicting Castro's claim to supervisors that he was engaged in a three-minute struggle for his life.
  • City surveillance video showed Sosa-Celis holding a snow shovel and dropping it before the shooting, contradicting the federal narrative that he had attacked Castro with a shovel or broom. No part of the video showed anyone attacking Castro with the shovel.
  • The Justice Department had previously charged Sosa-Celis with assaulting law enforcement based on Castro's account, then dropped those charges after DHS admitted two agents had made untruthful statements.
  • DHS called Castro's arrest 'unlawful' and a 'political stunt,' asserting the matter should be handled at the federal level. DHS stated the U.S. Attorney's Office was investigating the false statements.
  • Moriarty stated: 'Mr. Castro is an ICE agent but his federal badge does not make him immune from state charges for his criminal conduct in Minnesota.'
  • Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison stated Castro's alleged shooting 'appears unwarranted, as evidenced by the lies Castro told his ICE supervisors to justify his unlawful actions.'
"Christian Castro's alleged shooting of Julio Sosa-Celis appears unwarranted, as evidenced by the lies Castro told his ICE supervisors to justify his unlawful actions," Minnesota AG Keith Ellison said.
mediaPlaceholder: City surveillance footage showing the 12-second confrontation and snow shovel being dropped before the shooting
